source_strategy: use a separate attitudes-and-values survey unrelated to the holdout LISS-based paper

population_definition.target_population: cross-cultural respondents represented through WVS wave 7 and demographic-value benchmark resources
population_definition.sample_design: development-only benchmark using non-holdout WVS-derived persona-alignment studies
population_definition.unit_of_analysis: cultural configuration or benchmark question response
population_definition.notes: Favor stable values, WVS-based demographic group patterns, and cross-cultural structure rather than event-specific opinions.

example_constructs: religiosity and traditional versus secular-rational orientation

reported_findings_template: alignment with WVS cultural profiles by demographic group

human_reference_summary.source_paper: Culturally Grounded Personas in Large Language Models
human_reference_summary.reported_sample_or_scale: 93,312 cultural configurations; WVB-Probe with 36 value questions and 3 demographic variables
key_results: personas are built from WVS-derived cultural variables
evaluation targets demographic-level consistency with World Values Survey group patterns
benchmark probes continent, residential area, and education level
focus is cross-cultural structure and moral/value variation
